title: panicum hirticaule subsp. sonorum
intro: Panicum hirticaule subsp. sonorum, also known as Sonoran Panic Grass, is a subspecies of grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin irritations, and other minor health issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially aiding in the management of diabetes. Sonoran Panic Grass can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for herbal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and optimal preparations.
